# Trigonometry for height and distance

## Short Teaching Summary
To find the height (h) when given the horizontal distance (d) and the angle of elevation, use the formula tan(angle) = h/d.

## Difficulty
0.20

## Prerequisites
- Right triangle setup

## Related Concepts
- Trigonometry for height and distance (example_of)
- Right triangle setup (requires)

## Misconceptions
- None recorded.

## Best Examples
- To find the height (h) from the ground when given the angle of elevation and horizontal distance (d), use the formula tan theta = h/d.
- To find the distance (d) when given the angle of elevation and height (h), use the formula d = h/tan theta.
- If the angle of elevation is 60 degrees from a nearer point, the distance (d) can be found using the formula d = h/sqrt(3).

## Best Analogy
- None recorded.
